topic: Orientation, embryology, and congenital heart defects
notes: Welcome and course logistics (Step 1 overview, daily Mon–Sun flow, FA + UWorld pacing, dashboard walkthrough). Cardiac embryology and fetal/postnatal circulation. Congenital heart defects (5 T's; VSD/ASD/PDA, Eisenmenger), coarctation of the aorta, persistent pulmonary HTN of the newborn, defect-syndrome associations. UWorld questions.
Chambers, surface anatomy, CT/CXR correlates, pericardial anatomy. UWorld questions on cardiac anatomy.
Cardiac output (variables and equations), cardiac and vascular function curves, pressure-volume loops, the cardiac cycle. UWorld questions on cardiac physiology.
Splitting of S2, full auscultation map, systolic and diastolic murmurs, maneuvers that change their intensity. UWorld questions.

topic: ECG, action potentials, and BP control
notes: Myocyte + pacemaker action potentials, ECG (narrow- and wide-complex arrhythmias, conduction blocks), baroreceptor and chemoreceptor reflexes, normal cardiac pressures, autoregulation by organ, capillary fluid exchange (Starling), atrial fib/flutter, ventricular arrhythmias, torsades, conduction blocks. UWorld questions.

topic: Coronary artery disease, IHD, and myocardial infarction
notes: Coronary vessels, CAD, IHD manifestations, MI evolution and histology, biomarkers, ECG localisation by lead, MI complications. UWorld questions.

Hypertension, hyperlipidemia signs, atherosclerosis pathogenesis, arteriolosclerosis (hyaline + hyperplastic), abdominal and thoracic aortic aneurysms, aortic dissection (Stanford A vs B), subclavian steal. UWorld questions.

DCM, HCM, RCM, takotsubo, peripartum cardiomyopathy. Left and right heart failure (HFrEF / HFpEF). Shock (hypovolemic, cardiogenic, obstructive, distributive). Sudden cardiac death and syncope. UWorld questions.

Acute rheumatic fever, rheumatic heart disease, infective endocarditis (acute, subacute, Libman-Sacks, marantic), acute pericarditis + tamponade, myocarditis, cardiac tumors (myxoma, rhabdomyoma, mets), large/medium/small vessel vasculitides, vascular tumors. UWorld questions.

Hypertension treatment by comorbidity, ACE inhibitors / ARBs, β-blockers, calcium channel blockers (CCBs), hydralazine, ranolazine, sacubitril/valsartan, lipid-lowering agents (statins, fibrates, niacin, ezetimibe, PCSK9, BAS), digoxin, antiarrhythmics (Class I A/B/C, II, III, IV, adenosine, magnesium), pulmonary hypertension drugs. UWorld questions.

Lung development phases (embryonic, pseudoglandular, canalicular, saccular, alveolar), bronchial branching, alveolar cell types (Type I/II, club cells), surfactant, congenital anomalies (TE fistula, pulmonary hypoplasia). UWorld questions.
Conducting vs respiratory zones, bronchopulmonary segments, lung relations, diaphragm + accessory muscles of breathing, pleural anatomy. UWorld questions.

TLC/FRC/VC/RV, important pulmonary equations, lung-chest wall mechanics, compliance and hysteresis, surface tension and Laplace's law, oxygen-hemoglobin dissociation curve, methemoglobin and carboxyhemoglobin. UWorld questions.

O₂ content equation, A–a gradient, oxygen delivery, V/Q ratios across the lung and how shunt vs dead space behave, pulmonary circulation, hypoxic pulmonary vasoconstriction, response to high altitude. UWorld questions.

topic: Control of breathing and sleep apnea
notes: Central + peripheral chemoreceptors, ventilation control, response to exercise, hypoxia, hypercapnia. Breathing patterns (Cheyne-Stokes, Kussmaul, ataxic). Sleep apnea (obstructive vs central). UWorld questions.

Upper-airway conditions (rhinosinusitis, deviated septum, epistaxis, head and neck cancer). Obstructive lung disease — asthma, COPD (emphysema and chronic bronchitis), bronchiectasis. UWorld questions.

Restrictive lung disease (intrinsic vs extrinsic), idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis, sarcoidosis, pneumoconioses, hypersensitivity pneumonitis, ARDS, neonatal RDS, pulmonary embolism, pulmonary edema, pulmonary hypertension. Pneumonia overview (lobar, bronchopneumonia, interstitial), specific pneumonias by organism, lung abscess. UWorld questions.

topic: TB, lung cancer, and respiratory pharmacology
notes: Tuberculosis (primary, secondary, miliary, latent), fungal pneumonias, pleural diseases (pneumothorax, effusion, mesothelioma, hemothorax), mediastinal masses, lung cancer (small cell vs non-small cell, paraneoplastic syndromes, Pancoast tumor). Respiratory pharmacology — asthma drugs (β2 agonists, ICS, LTRAs, biologics), COPD drugs, methylxanthines, antitussives, decongestants, antihistamines. UWorld questions.

topic: Study designs, levels of evidence, and diagnostic testing
notes: Observational vs experimental designs: case reports, case series, cross-sectional, case-control, cohort, RCTs, meta-analyses. Levels of evidence pyramid. Clinical trial phases (I–IV). Diagnostic test characteristics: 2×2 tables, sensitivity, specificity, positive and negative predictive values (PPV/NPV), likelihood ratios, prevalence effects, ROC curves, pre- and post-test probability, Bayes' theorem. Screening principles and worked examples (troponins, D-dimer, common screening tests). UWorld questions.

topic: Quantifying risk, demographic transitions, and survival analysis
notes: Risk quantification: incidence, prevalence, relative risk (RR), odds ratio (OR), absolute risk reduction (ARR), number needed to treat (NNT) and NNH, attributable risk. Demographic transition theory and the epidemiologic transition through the stages of population health. Survival analysis: Kaplan-Meier curves, log-rank test, Cox proportional hazards / hazard ratios, censoring. UWorld questions.

Types of bias and how to minimize them — selection bias, recall bias, observer/measurement bias, lead-time bias, length-time bias, attrition bias, Hawthorne effect, publication bias. Confounding: definition, recognition, control strategies (randomization, restriction, matching, stratification, multivariable adjustment). Effect modification (interaction) — how it differs from confounding and how to detect it. UWorld questions.

topic: Statistical distributions, statistical testing, and confidence intervals
notes: Statistical distributions — normal/Gaussian, binomial, Poisson, t-distribution; measures of central tendency and dispersion; standard error vs standard deviation. Statistical testing: hypothesis testing, p-values, type I (α) and type II (β) errors, statistical power. Confidence intervals — construction and interpretation. Common tests by data type (t-test, paired t, ANOVA, chi-squared, Fisher's exact, correlation and regression). Interpreting forest plots and meta-analyses. UWorld questions.
Hypertrophy, hyperplasia, atrophy, metaplasia, dysplasia. Reversible vs irreversible cell injury. Intrinsic and extrinsic apoptosis pathways. UWorld questions.

Necrosis types (coagulative, liquefactive, caseous, fat, fibrinoid, gangrenous). Ischemia and infarcts (red vs pale). Free radical injury, oxidative stress, and antioxidant defences. UWorld questions.

Acute inflammation cardinal signs, vasodilation and increased permeability, leukocyte margination, rolling, adhesion, transmigration. Inflammatory mediators (histamine, bradykinin, complement, cytokines, prostaglandins, leukotrienes). Exudate vs transudate. UWorld questions.

Chronic inflammation cell types and outcomes. Granulomatous inflammation (caseating vs non-caseating, causes, Th1/IFN-γ axis). Tissue repair phases — hemostasis, inflammation, proliferation, remodelling. Wound healing by primary, secondary, tertiary intention. Scar formation (hypertrophic vs keloid), fibrosis. UWorld questions.

Neoplastic progression (dysplasia → carcinoma in situ → invasive). Tumor nomenclature (benign vs malignant, epithelial vs mesenchymal). Hallmarks of cancer. Tumor grade vs stage. Common metastases by primary site. UWorld questions.

topic: Neoplasia II — oncogenes and tumor suppressors
notes: Oncogenes (RAS, MYC, HER2, BCR-ABL, etc.) and tumor suppressors (Rb, p53, BRCA, APC, NF1/NF2, VHL, MEN1). Inherited cancer syndromes. Cancer epidemiology by site. Paraneoplastic syndromes (endocrine, neurologic, dermatologic, hematologic). UWorld questions.

topic: Neoplasia III — tumor markers and aging
notes: Tumor markers (AFP, CA-125, CEA, PSA, CA 19-9, β-hCG, etc.), key immunohistochemical stains, oncogenic microbes (HPV, EBV, HBV/HCV, HTLV-1, H. pylori, HHV-8), chemical and environmental carcinogens. Physiologic aging changes. UWorld questions.
Whole-chapter review: cellular injury, inflammation, neoplasia, aging — integrated across organ systems. Mixed UWorld block with debrief.
